title: Orthostatic BP documentation

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting postural dizziness or falls context, lying or seated and standing blood pressure measurements, timing, symptoms, and clinician review without diagnosing orthostatic hypotension or changing treatment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: NG136 directly supports clinician documentation of postural symptoms and lying or seated and standing blood-pressure measurements while leaving diagnosis and treatment entirely clinician-entered.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting postural dizziness or falls context, lying or seated and standing blood pressure measurements, timing, symptoms, and clinician review without diagnosing orthostatic hypotension or changing treatment.

source.title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.8 — postural hypotension symptoms and standing blood pressure

sources_used.title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management

source_limitations: Body position, rest period, exact timing, blood-pressure and pulse values, symptoms during measurement, hydration, medicines, falls, autonomic or neurological context, clinician interpretation, diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
